content: We can frame Self-Refine, the task-specific output refinement framework, as a concrete 'workflow implementation' of abstract LLM self-reflection mechanisms, and multi-perspective reflection designs from Topic B can be embedded into each iteration step of Self-Refine to resolve its blind spot of one-dimensional self-feedback. This hybrid framework would retain Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no additional training or supervised data while cutting self-confirmation bias that plagues current unsupervised self-refinement.
